enriched_points.expanded_content: Respecting autonomy entails recognizing patients' rights to make informed and reflective choices regarding their treatment, especially in critical neurological conditions.
Informed consent is crucial in neuro-ethics, ensuring patients understand their options and the implications of their decisions, thus empowering them in their care.
Practicing autonomy can be complex, with challenges such as assessing competence, cultural beliefs, and balancing paternalism versus patient rights in healthcare settings.
